wordlist.desc: A beak is the hard curved part of a bird’s mouth.
wordlist.exam: The bird used her beak to dig a small hole in the ground.

wordlist.desc: If something is damp, it is somewhat wet.
wordlist.exam: My hair is still damp from my shower.

wordlist.desc: To disapprove of something is to think that it is wrong.
wordlist.exam: Alex, disapproved of his son’s behavior.

wordlist.desc: You use except to talk about the only thing that a statement does not apply to.
wordlist.exam: Everyone had fun on the trip except Jim, who felt ill the whole time.

wordlist.desc: A flight is an act of flying, often in an airplane.
wordlist.exam: The airplane made a flight around the world.

wordlist.desc: When someone is fond of something, they like it.
wordlist.exam: I am very fond of chocolate cake.

wordlist.desc: When something or someone is immoral, they are evil or do bad things.
wordlist.exam: Stealing money from one’s mother is an immoral act.

wordlist.desc: Ivy is a plant with long vines that grows upward typically on walls.
wordlist.exam: The walls of the castle are covered with ivy.

wordlist.desc: To moan is to make a low sound when feeling pain or sadness.
wordlist.exam: The sick man moaned from his bed.

wordlist.desc: If someone is oblivious of something, they are unaware of it.
wordlist.exam: I was late to school because I was oblivious of the time.

wordlist.desc: To perish means to die, usually because of bad conditions or bad events.
wordlist.exam: Many people perish in wars.

wordlist.desc: A pit is a big hole in the ground.
wordlist.exam: Michael fell into the pit and needed some help to get out.

wordlist.desc: The rim is the outside edge of a round thing, like a cup or bottle.
wordlist.exam: I put my lips to the rim of the bottle and took a drink.

wordlist.desc: A roost is a place where birds can land and rest or sleep.
wordlist.exam: The family of doves made a roost at the top of the tree.

wordlist.desc: If something is slippery, it is wet or smooth and causes people to slip.
wordlist.exam: The repairman fell down on the floor because it was too slippery.

wordlist.desc: To soar means to fly high in the air.
wordlist.exam: As the sun rose, two eagles soared through the sky.

wordlist.desc: If something is trivial, it is not important or meaningful.
wordlist.exam: Television is trivial, but learning to read is very important.

wordlist.desc: If something is typical, it is normal for a certain type of thing.
wordlist.exam: Barking is typical behavior for dogs.

wordlist.desc: If something is utterly a way, it is completely that way.
wordlist.exam: When the sun was blocked by the moon, it was utterly dark outside.

wordlist.desc: To weep means to cry.
wordlist.exam: My sister told me the dramatic movie would make me weep.

exercise.story:
Choose the answer that best fits the question.

  1. What does a sad movie cause people to do?
    • a. To weep
    • b. To except
    • c. To perish
    • d. To disapprove
  2. Where would a bird build a home?
    • a. On a rim
    • b. In a pit
    • c. On a roost
    • d. On a flight
  3. Which word best describes a wet towel?
    • a. Immoral
    • b. Damp
    • c. Trivial
    • d. Fond
  4. What does it mean to fly?
    • a. To soar
    • b. To moan
    • c. To be oblivious
    • d. To be typical
  5. Which word would best describe a smooth, wet stone?
    • a. Ivy
    • b. Utterly
    • c. Slippery
    • d. Immoral
  6. Which word describes something that would have a rim?
    • a. A thought
    • b. A cup
    • c. A person
    • d. A smell
  7. Which word describes something that has a beak?
    • a. A bird
    • b. A boat
    • c. A car
    • d. A lion
  8. What is typical about fish?
    • a. They can swim.
    • b. One type can sing.
    • c. They can live on land.
    • d. Some can live for close to 50 years.
  9. Which of these people would most likely be moaning?
    • a. A doctor
    • b. A happy boy
    • c. A wife
    • d. A hurt woman
  10. Which person has been proven to have committed an immoral act?
    • a. A teacher
    • b. A criminal
    • c. A judge
    • d. A fireman

Choose the word that is the better fit for each blank.

  1. oblivious / perish
    All of the people in the movie will __________ if they remain __________ of the monster.
  2. roost / fond
    When the owl wants to make a __________ , it is __________ of using an empty barn.
  3. pit / rim
    He grabbed the can by its __________ , then tossed it far down into the __________.
  4. flight / soars
    The eagle __________ into the clouds when it goes on a long __________.
  5. moan / weep
    Every time the sick woman would __________ in pain, her daughter would __________.
  6. immoral / typical
    It is not __________ for a policeman to do __________ things like stealing.
  7. beak / except Every part of the bird was beautiful, __________ for its short, ugly __________.
  8. trivial / disapprove
    Because his assistant is teaching them __________ plays, the coach will __________ of the time wasted.
  9. utterly / ivy
    Because it __________ covered the fence, the gardener had to remove all of the __________.
  10. slippery / damp
    The ground became dangerous and __________ because everyone placed their __________ towels there.

Exercise 1
  1. a
  2. c
  3. b
  4. a
  5. c
  6. b
  7. a
  8. a
  9. d
  10. b
Exercise 2
  1. perish / oblivious
  2. roost / fond
  3. rim / pit
  4. soars / flight
  5. moan / weep
  6. typical / immoral
  7. except / beak
  8. trivial / disapprove
  9. utterly / ivy
  10. slippery / damp

reading.story:
There was a fox that lived in the forest. Fox loved to play mean tricks on the other animals. One day, he used the sharp rim of a bottle to dig a pit in the ground. He hid in a tree until Rabbit came to the pit’s edge. Then, he jumped out and pushed Rabbit into the pit. Fox laughed and ran away. The angry Rabbit climbed out and told the other animals what happened.

The others said, “That is typical behavior for Fox. He does mean things all the time. Sometimes, he is completely immoral. We all disapprove of his actions, so we should teach him a lesson. Tomorrow, we’ll push Fox into that pit.”

The next day, all of the animals hid near the pit and waited for Fox. Fox was oblivious to the hidden animals. He walked up to the pit to see if Rabbit was still trapped. Just then, the other animals ran up to Fox and pushed him in. All the animals laughed and cheered, except Fox, of course.

Fox couldn’t get out! The walls of the pit were covered in damp ivy. It was too slippery for him to climb out. He was utterly helpless. He moaned and began to weep.

At last, he saw Eagle watching him from her roost. He yelled, “Eagle, please help me! If I don’t get out of here, I will perish!”

Eagle said, “You may think your tricks are trivial, but you hurt others when you do mean things. I’ll help you if you promise to be nice.”

Fox said, “I promise!”

Eagle began her flight to the bottom of the pit. She picked up Fox with her beak and soared out of the pit. She dropped Fox safely on the ground.

Fox thanked Eagle and kept his promise. He was nice to the other animals. The animals even became fond of Fox, and the forest was happy place.

Part A: Mark each statement T for true or F for false. Rewrite the false statements to make them true.

  1. _____ Fox used the rim of a bottle to dig a deep pit.
    __________
  2. _____ Fox had a roost near the pit.
    __________
  3. _____ The damp ivy was too slippery for Fox to climb out.
    __________
  4. _____ Immoral and mean behavior was typical with Fox.
    __________
  5. _____ Fox was oblivious to the hidden pit.
    __________
  6. _____ Eagle flew into the pit to save Fox.
    __________

Part B: Answer the questions.

  1. How did Rabbit fall into the pit?
    • a. Rabbit ran into the pit.
    • b. Fox pushed him into the pit.
    • c. Rabbit sat on the edge.
    • d. Fox laughed and ran away.
  2. Why did Fox thank Eagle?
    • a. She gave him money.
    • b. She became fond of Fox.
    • c. She did not let Fox perish.
    • d. She gave Fox some rope to climb out.
  3. Why did Fox moan and weep?
    • a. The animals taught him a lesson.
    • b. He saw Eagle in her roost.
    • c. He felt trivial.
    • d. He felt utterly helpless.
  4. What happened after Eagle’s flight to the bottom of the pit?
    • a. She disapproved of Fox.
    • b. She got stuck in the pit.
    • c. She made Fox promise to leave the forest.
    • d. She picked up Fox with her beak.

Reading Comprehension
Part A
  1. T
  2. F / Eagle had a roost near the pit.
  3. T
  4. T
  5. F / Fox was oblivious to the hidden animals.
  6. T
Part B
  1. b
  2. c
  3. d
  4. d
